source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Leo has built a stick made up of 27 building blocks.
<image1>
He splits the stick into two pieces in a way so that one part is twice as long as the other. He keeps repeating this again and again. He takes one of the two pieces and splits it up so that one piece is twice as long as the other. Which of the following pieces can never result in this way?
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field contains only labels A-E; option contents are not provided as text

issues: Option contents are not provided as structured text; metadata notes that only labels A-E are available in the options field.
Question text contains image placeholders <image1> and <image2>, so full interpretation depends on the associated image asset.

image_dependency: asset_registry_record.issues notes that question text contains image placeholders and full interpretation depends on the image asset
choices only provide labels A-E, so the actual candidate pieces are not available in structured text
multi_step: Question describes a repeated split process with a 2:1 length constraint, indicating iterative reasoning rather than a single lookup
The task asks which piece can never result, which suggests evaluating possibility/impossibility across multiple resulting cases
Option contents are image-based and not structured in text, so the full reasoning likely combines textual rules with visual answer choices
verifiability: answer_present=true and answer_type=choice/option, so the final response format is discrete
registry_passed=true, indicating the sample assets were registered successfully
Verifiability is reduced because option contents are not provided as structured text and interpretation depends on the image

risk_flags: Answer label alone is hard to independently verify without access to the visual options
Option contents are not provided as structured text; only labels A-E are available

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text exists, a primary image asset exists, and an answer is present.
The sample is strongly image-dependent, but the required image asset is available, so the core semantics appear recoverable.
Initial scoring is solid overall, with moderate-to-good multi-step and verifiability scores.

There is some follow-up risk because option contents are not structured in text and the question includes image placeholders, but these issues do not remove the core semantic support needed for collection.
